name: Bienservida y pico de la Sarga — circular por la sierra de Alcaraz
region_intro: Mountain biking around Bienservida offers diverse landscapes and an extensive network of routes, situated near the Natural Park of Calares del Río Mundo and La Sima. The terrain features challenging ascents through olive groves and pine forests, with significant elevation gains. Riders can explore areas alongside the Río de la Mesta and encounter prominent peaks like Pico Almenara and Pico de la Sarga. The region provides a mix of paved and unpaved segments, catering to mountain bikers seeking varied conditions. Best mountain bike trails around Bienservida The most popular mountain bike trail is El Monte Padrón desde Villaverde de Guadalimar — circular por el Parque natural de Los Calares del Mundo y de la Sima, a 27.5 miles (44.2 km) difficult trail that takes 5 hours 0 minutes to complete. This route explores the natural park, offering challenging climbs and descents. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Bienservida y pico de la Sarga — circular por la sierra de Alcaraz, a difficult 21.8 miles (35.1 km) path. This route provides panoramic views from Pico de la Sarga across the Sierra de Alcaraz and surrounding areas. Local mountain bikers also love the Piedra del Cambrón Loop Trail, a 15.5 miles (24.9 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 3 minutes. This difficult loop offers a demanding ride with significant elevation. Mountain biking around Bienservida is defined by challenging ascents to peaks like Pico de la Sarga, routes alongside the Río de la Mesta, and trails through olive groves and pine forests. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Bienservida are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from a few reviews. More than 260 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Bienservida's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Bienservida?
A: There are nearly 20 mountain bike routes around Bienservida, offering a variety of experiences. These include 2 easy, 6 moderate, and 11 difficult trails, catering to different skill levels.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Bienservida?
A: While many trails in Bienservida are challenging, there are a couple of easier routes suitable for families or those looking for a less strenuous ride. For instance, the area offers routes with mostly paved surfaces, allowing for a more relaxed experience amidst the natural scenery.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Bienservida?
A: Bienservida's mountain bike trails feature diverse terrain, including challenging ascents through olive groves and pine forests, significant elevation gains, and segments alongside the Río de la Mesta. You'll encounter a mix of paved and unpaved surfaces, with some routes requiring good fitness and potentially some bike pushing.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in the Bienservida area?
A: Yes, many routes in Bienservida are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1212220196/el-monte-padron-desde-villaverde-de-guadalimar-circular-por-el-parque-natural-de-los-calares-del-mundo-y-de-la-sima" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Monte Padrón desde Villaverde de Guadalimar — circular por el Parque natural de Los Calares del Mundo y de la Sima&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 44.2 km loop, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1040827487/bienservida-y-pico-de-la-sarga-circular-por-la-sierra-de-alcaraz" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bienservida y pico de la Sarga — circular por la sierra de Alcaraz&lt;/a&gt; is a 35.1 km difficult circular path.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions or viewpoints along the mountain bike trails?
A: The region offers several impressive natural features. You can find panoramic views from &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3933686" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almenara Peak&lt;/a&gt; and Pico de la Sarga. The area also features the beautiful La Pileta valley with its waterfall, and the El Nacimiento del Río La Madre, where the river forms pools. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1040827487/bienservida-y-pico-de-la-sarga-circular-por-la-sierra-de-alcaraz" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bienservida y pico de la Sarga — circular por la sierra de Alcaraz&lt;/a&gt; route specifically provides panoramic views from Pico de la Sarga.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or castles to explore near the mountain bike routes?
A: Yes, the area around Bienservida has historical points of interest. You can find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4577208" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de la Yedra Ruins&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6536732" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Keep Tower of Hornos de Segura&lt;/a&gt;. The San Cristóbal Hermitage, overlooking the Río de la Mesta, also offers a scenic point of interest along some trails.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Bienservida?
A: The mountain bike trails in Bienservida are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from a few reviews. Riders often praise the challenging ascents to peaks like Pico de la Sarga, the diverse landscapes including olive groves and pine forests, and the refreshing presence of the Río de la Mesta alongside many paths.

Q: Are there any trails that follow rivers or have water features?
A: Yes, the Río de la Mesta accompanies many paths in the area, providing a refreshing element, especially during warmer months. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1212229854/cotillas-circular-route-along-the-guadalimar-river" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cotillas — circular route along the Guadalimar river&lt;/a&gt; follows the Guadalimar river, offering scenic views along the water.

Q: What is the longest mountain bike trail available in Bienservida?
A: The longest mountain bike trail in the area is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31462299" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mundo River crossing – Technical ascent and descent loop from Almenara&lt;/a&gt;, which spans approximately 71.2 km and involves significant elevation gain, making it a challenging full-day ride.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Bienservida?
A: Bienservida's diverse landscapes and consistently green environment make it appealing for mountain biking. While specific best times can vary, the presence of rivers like the Río de la Mesta offers a refreshing element, particularly during warmer months. Consider spring and autumn for milder temperatures, though the region's natural beauty can be enjoyed year-round.

Q: Are there any mountain bike routes that offer views of the wider region?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1040827487/bienservida-y-pico-de-la-sarga-circular-por-la-sierra-de-alcaraz" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bienservida y pico de la Sarga — circular por la sierra de Alcaraz&lt;/a&gt; route provides impressive panoramic views from Pico de la Sarga, encompassing the Sierra de Alcaraz, Sierra del Segura, Campos de Montiel, and the Natural Park of Cazorla, Segura y las Villas.
tips: Bienservida is one of the entrances to the forests, valleys and mountains of the Sierra de Alcaraz and Calar del Mundo. The town is surrounded by beautiful natural landscapes and is also one of the stages of the Don Quixote Route. The mountain bike route leaves the urban complex towards Villaverde de Guadalimar and after one kilometer it turns to the right along the path of the source of the Turruchel river, a recreational area with fountains, springs, pools and small waterfalls.
It is the second highest peak (1780 meters) of the Alcaraz mountain range and a panoramic balcony of the surrounding mountains and the fabulous Calar del Mundo.
